fields.title: Clean Code
fields.author: Robert C. Martin
fields.description: A Handbook of Agile Software Craftsmanship. Even bad code can function. But if code isn't clean, it can bring a development organization to its knees.

fields.title: Design Patterns
fields.author: Gang of Four
fields.description: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software. Capturing a wealth of experience about the design of object-oriented software.

fields.title: The Pragmatic Programmer
fields.author: David Thomas, Andrew Hunt
fields.description: Your Journey to Mastery. A guide to becoming a better programmer through practical advice.

fields.title: Introduction to Algorithms
fields.author: Thomas H. Cormen
fields.description: The bible of algorithms. Comprehensive introduction to modern algorithms.

fields.title: Python Crash Course
fields.author: Eric Matthes
fields.description: A Hands-On, Project-Based Introduction to Programming. Fast-paced introduction to Python.

fields.title: JavaScript: The Good Parts
fields.author: Douglas Crockford
fields.description: Unearthing the Excellence in JavaScript. Most programming languages contain good and bad parts.

fields.title: Database System Concepts
fields.author: Abraham Silberschatz
fields.description: Comprehensive treatment of the fundamentals of database systems.

fields.title: Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach
fields.author: Stuart Russell, Peter Norvig
fields.description: The leading textbook in Artificial Intelligence used in universities worldwide.

fields.title: Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach
fields.author: James Kurose
fields.description: Building on the successful top-down approach, this textbook continues with an early emphasis on application-layer paradigms.

fields.title: Head First Design Patterns
fields.author: Eric Freeman, Elisabeth Robson
fields.description: Building Extensible and Maintainable Object-Oriented Software. A brain-friendly guide to design patterns.
